At 1235 B/15, east of Alice Point, in approximate position 39°25'N, 17°22'E, a small two-masted schooner was sighted bearing 200° at a range of 7000 yards. Enemy course was 060°.
At 1320 B/15, a second small two-masted schooner was sighted bearing 220° at a range of 10000 yards. Enemy course was 020°.
The following day HMS Upright still patrolled east of Alice Point retiring to patrol further to the east after surfacing. (45)

19 Feb 1942 At 0250 B/19, HMS Upright (Lt. J.W.D. Coombe, RN) received the Capt. S.10's signal timed 0155 B/19 which reported that Upright's current patrol position was compromised. Course was altered to the south.
Later in the day course was altered to close the coast and patrol near Alice Point again. (45)

20 Feb 19421400 B/20, HMS Upright (Lt. J.W.D. Coombe, RN) reveived the Capt. S.10's signal timed 1125 B/20 which contained the following orders; Patrol off Cape Spartivento on 21 February and return to Malta on 23 February proceeding through positions 37°12' 16°02'E and 36°20'N, 15° 20'E. (45)

21 Feb 1942 Between 1900 B/21 and 2010 B/21, 23 bombs and depth charges were heard to explode. Some being sufficiently close to shake HMS Upright (Lt. J.W.D. Coombe, RN) and cause the needles of the depth gauges to jump. It appeared probable that HMS Upright had been sighted by aircraft during the afternoon and that an A/S hunt was in progress.
At 1952 B/21, when in position 169° - Cape Spartivento - 7.5 miles and about to surface when HE was picked up on bearing 260° and closing.
At 1958 B/21, more HE was picked up on bearing 260°. It was thought both sets of HE picked up was coming from destroyers.

Until 2149 B/21, there was continuous HE between 280° and 080°. Sometimes it was closing and sometimes it was fading to nil. One fast reciprocating engine was heard at 2022 B/21 which was thought to be a trawler.
At 2054 B/21, HMS Upright came to periscope depth. Nothing was in sight though.
At 2154 B/21, HMS Upright surfaced. There was nothing in sight and no more HE was heard.
At 2200 B/21, course was set for Malta.
The following movements of Italian ships were taking place in this area on this day [for so far we could find out]:
The Italian torpedo boat Generale Marcello Prestinari sailed from Messina for an A/S sweep at 0530 B/21 and returned at 2125 B/21.

At 0345 B/21, the Italian transport Unione (6071 GRT, built 1942) sailed from Naples at 0345 B/21 escorted by the Italian torpedo boats Orsa and Enrico Cosenz and transited the Straits of Messina at 1730 B/21.

At 1730 B/21-1915 B/21, the Italian transports Ravello (6142 GRT, built 1941) and Monginevro (5324 GRT, built 1940) sailed from Messina escorted by the Italian destroyers Ugolino Vivaldi, Lanzerotto Malocello, Premuda, Strale, Nicolò Zeno and the Italian torpedo boats Giuseppe Dezza and Pallade and merged with the Unione-convoy.
The Italian motor torpedo boats MAS 556 and MAS 562 sailed from Messina at 0735 B/21 for an A/S sweep and returned at 0245 B/22. (41)
23 Feb 1942 Around 0730 B/23, HMS Upright (Lt. J.W.D. Coombe, RN) ended her 24th war patrol (22nd in the Mediterranean) at Malta. (41)
3 Mar 1942 HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) was near missed by a bomb during an air raid causing some damage for which she was taken in hand for repairs the following day. (46)
18 Mar 1942 HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) completed her repairs. A trial dive was made on completion. (46)
19 Mar 1942 Around 0600 B/19, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) departed Malta for Gibraltar. She was to proceed to the U.K. where she was to refit. On board were only two Mk.II torpedoes.
She was to pass through position 38°10'N 09°00'E about 2200 B/22 and her further route would be signalled to her by F.O.C.N.A. (Flag Officer Commanding North Atlantic).

21 Mar 1942 In a signal timed 1140 A/21, F.O.C.N.A. (Flag Officer Commanding North Atlantic) sent the following order to HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN); From 38°10'N, 09°00'E proceed to arrive in position 'R', 38°10'N, 05°00'E at 2000 A/24. Further onward route will be signalled.

At 1405 B/21, south-west of Marsala, in approximate position 37°32'N, 12°13'E, HMS Upright sighted smoke and masts of a transport and destroyers bearing 310° at a range of 9 miles. [No course or speed is given in the logbook, there is no passage report available.]

This was were perhaps the Italian transport Monginevro (5324 GRT, built 1940), escorted by the Italian destroyer Premuda and the Italian torpedo boat Clio, which had sailed at 1845 B/20-1943 B/20 from Tripoli. Premuda was detached to Palermo, arriving at 2300 B/21, Monginevro and Clio arrived at Naples at 0924 B/22.
At 1630 B/21, a loud explosion was heard.

At 1920 B/21, west-south-west of Marsala, when in approximate position 37°39'N, 12°07'E, two sailing vessels (barques) were sighted bearing 030° at a range of 3 miles. Enemy course was 330° at 4 knots. (47)

23 Mar 1942 In a signal 1808 A/23, F.O.C.N.A. (Flag Officer Commanding North Atlantic) sent the following orders to HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN); Pass through position 'S', 38°10'N, 00°30'E at 2200 B/26 and through position 'T', 36°30'N, 02°00'W at 0600 A/28. (48)
29 Mar 1942 Around 1700 A/29, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) arrived at Gibraltar from Malta. (46)
2 Apr 1942 Around 1430 A/2, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) departed Gibraltar for Holy Loch.
On 30 March, two more Mk.2 torpedoes had been loaded so there was now a full salvo available in case it was needed.

She was to pass through position 'A', 43°00'N 11°20'E at 0800 A/8. Further onward routing was provided F.O.S. (Flag Officer Submarines). She was to proceeded through positions 'B', 45°22'N, 09°46'E and 'C', 47°36'N, 08°12'E to make rendezvous as will be ordered by the Capt. S.3 in the vicinity Bishops Rock on 13 April. (49)

9 Apr 1942 In a signal timed 1445 B/9, the Capt. S.3 sent the following orders to HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN). Make rendezvous with HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) in position 180° - Bishop Rock - 3 miles at 0700 B/13 for onward passage to Holy Loch. H.Q.C.C. is requested to institute total bombing restrictions within an area of radius 20 miles from Bishop Rock from 0400 B to 1600 B/13. (50)
13 Apr 1942 Around 0700 B/15, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) made rendezvous off Bishops Rock with HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) that was to escort her to Holy Loch. (49)
15 Apr 1942 Around 0800 B/15, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) arrived at Holy Loch from Gibraltar. (49)
18 Apr 1942 Around 1700 B/18, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) departed Holy Loch for Blyth. She was escorted by HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR). (49)
21 Apr 1942 Around 1215 B/21, HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) arrived at Blyth. (49)
23 Apr 1942 HMS Upright (Lt. J.S. Wraith, DSC, RN) is taken in hand for refit at Blyth. She is docked down in No.4 graving dock. (49)
16 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) is undocked and ended her refit at Blyth. She was not to join an operational flotilla but would be used for training duties. (51)
18 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) conducted engine trials off Blyth. (51)
20 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) is wiped (degaussed) at Blyth. (51)
21 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) conducted D/G trials at Blyth. (51)
22 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) conducted D/G and compass adjustment trials at Blyth. (51)
23 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) conducted trials off Blyth. (51)
25 Jul 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) conducted diving trials off Blyth. (51)
27 Jul 1942 Around 2000 B/27, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) departed Blyth for Dundee. She was escorted by HMS Nettle (Skr. F. Dale, RNR). (51)
28 Jul 1942 Around 1240 B/28, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) arrived at Dundee from Blyth. (51)

1 Aug 1942 Around 1200 B/1, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow together with HMS P 54 (Lt. C.E. Oxborrow, DSC, RN) which was to proceed to Lerwick. They were escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (52)
2 Aug 1942 Around 1130 B/2, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow from Dundee. At Scapa Flow she was to participate in A/S exercises. (52)
3 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out independent exercises at Scapa Flow. (52)
4 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out independent exercises at Scapa Flow. (52)

5 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out D/F calibration trials at Scapa Flow which were followed by exercises off Scapa Flow with HMS Anson (Capt. H.R.G. Kinahan, CBE, RN, flying the flag of Vice-Admiral Sir B. Fraser, CB, KBE, RN) and her destroyer screen made up of the destroyers HMS Inglefield (Cdr. A.G. West, RN), HMS Windsor (Lt.Cdr. D.H.F. Hetherington, DSC, RN) and HMS Walpole (Lt. A.S. Pomeroy, RN). The battleship and her destroyer screen remained out for more exercises during the night. (53)

6 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out exercises off Scapa Flow with HMS Anson (Capt. H.R.G. Kinahan, CBE, RN, flying the flag of Vice-Admiral Sir B. Fraser, CB, KBE, RN) and her destroyer screen which was made up of HMS Inglefield (Cdr. A.G. West, RN), HMS Windsor (Lt.Cdr. D.H.F. Hetherington, DSC, RN), HMS Walpole (Lt. A.S. Pomeroy, RN) and HMS Panther (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Jocelyn, RN). This last destroyer had joined HMS Anson and the other three destroyers in the afternoon. (52)

7 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Panther (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Jocelyn, RN), HMS Worcester (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Juniper, RN), HMS Avon Vale (Lt.Cdr. P.A.R. Withers, DSO, RN), USS Emmons (T/Cdr. T.C. Ragan, USN) and HMS Sheffield (Capt. A.W. Clarke, RN). (52)
8 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out experimental A/S exercises off Scapa Flow during which 12 practice attacks were made. (52)

10 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Gleaner (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Hewitt, DSC, RN), HMAS Quiberon (Cdr. H.W.S. Browning, OBE, RN), HMS Panther (Lt.Cdr. R.W. Jocelyn, RN) and HMS Impulsive (Lt.Cdr. E.G. Roper, DSC, RN). (52)

11 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Avon Vale (Lt.Cdr. P.A.R. Withers, DSO, RN), HMS Impulsive (Lt.Cdr. E.G. Roper, DSC, RN), HMS Worcester (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Juniper, RN) and HMAS Quiberon (Cdr. H.W.S. Browning, OBE, RN). (52)

13 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HNoMS Eskdale (Lt.Cdr. S. Storheill), RHS Adrias (?), HMS Gleaner (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Hewitt, DSC, RN) and HMS Harrier (Cdr. A.D.H. Jay, DSC, RN). (52)

14 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Milne (Capt. I.M.R. Campbell, RN), HNoMS Eskdale (Lt.Cdr. S. Storheill) and RHS Kanaris (?). (52)
15 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out independent exercises at Scapa Flow. (52)
17 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Milne (Capt. I.M.R. Campbell, RN) and RHS Kanaris (?). (52)
18 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Inglefield (Cdr. A.G. West, RN) and HNoMS Eskdale (Lt.Cdr. S. Storheill). (52)
19 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Milne (Capt. I.M.R. Campbell, RN) and HMS Windor (Lt.Cdr. D.H.F. Hetherington, RN). (52)
20 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out independet exercises at Scapa Flow. These were followed by A/S exercises with Motor Launches. (52)
22 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) carried out independent exercises at Scapa Flow. (52)
23 Aug 1942 Around 0830 A/23, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) departed Scapa Flow for Dundee.

At 0910 A/23, in the Pentland Firth she joined the Free French submarine FFS Rubis (Lt.Cdr. H.L.G. Rousselot) which came from Holy Loch. They were escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (52)
24 Aug 1942 Around 0745 A/24, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) arrived at Dundee. (52)
25 Aug 1942 Around 1810 A/25, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) departed Dundee for Blyth. She was escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (52)
26 Aug 1942 Around 0815 A/26, HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) arrived at Blyth. At Blyth she was deployed for the training of new submarine crews. (52)
27 Aug 1942 HMS Upright (Lt.Cdr. A.F. Collett, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth training a class of new submariners. (54)

5 Feb 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. P.R.H. Harrison, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) commenced a refit at Blyth. (68)
1 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) ended her refit at Blyth. She conducted engine trials and also a basis dive. (69)
2 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. (69)

7 Jun 1944 Around 0700 B/7, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) departed Blyth for Dundee where she arrived around 2000 B/7. She was escorted by HMS ML 229 (T/Lt. W.C.R. Walsh, RNVR). (69)
8 Jun 1944 Around 1320 B/8, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) departed Dundee for Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS Sardonyx (A/Lt.Cdr. T.A. Easton, RNVR). (69)
10 Jun 1944 Around 0440 B/10, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) arrived at Tobermory.
During the afternoon A/S exercises were carried out with HMS Bulldog (Lt.Cdr. C.G. Walker, RN) and HMCS St. Thomas (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.P. Denny, RCNR). (69)
11 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) carried out A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMCS St. Thomas (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.P. Denny, RCNR). (69)
12 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) carried out A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMCS St. Thomas (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.P. Denny, RCNR). (69)
13 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) carried out A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMCS St. Thomas (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.P. Denny, RCNR). (69)
16 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) proceeded from Tobermory to Oban. She was escorted by HMS Clorinde (T/Lt. M.L. Patterson, RNVR). (69)

17 Jun 1944 Around 0705 B/17, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) departed Oban for Rothesay where she arrived around 2145 B/17. She was escorted by HMS Kihna (A/Cdr.(Retd.) A.R.W. Sayle, RD, RNR). (69)

20 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area. These were followed by attack exercises during which HMS Kihna (A/Cdr.(Retd.) A.R.W. Sayle, RD, RNR) acted as target. (69)
21 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted D/F calibration trials in the Clyde area. These were followed by night exercises with HMS PC 74 (A/Lt.Cdr. A. Richardson, RNR). (69)

22 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) returned to Rothesay on completion of the night exercises. During these exercises HMS Upright had acted as target for HMS Safari (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN) which was conducting attack exercises for the C.O.Q.C. (Commanding Officers Qualifying Course). (69)
23 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area on a target towed by HMS Blade (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR). (69)

25 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) proceeded from Rothesay to Campbeltown. En-route she ran speed trails on the Arran measured mile and acted as target for HMS Unruffled (Lt. R.F. Park, RN) which was conducting attack exercises. (69)
26 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (69)

30 Jun 1944 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) returned to Campbeltown on completion of the night exercises. (69)

14 Aug 1944 For the daily positions of HMS Upright during the passage from Rothesay to St. Johns see the map below.

14 Aug 1944 Around 1800 B/14, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN), HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) departed Rothesay under escort of HMS Milford (Lt.Cdr. G.G. Slade, RN).

The next day, around 1250 B/15, HMS United and HMS Upright parted company to cross the Atlantic and proceed to St.Johns, Newfoundland. They were escorted by HMS Northern Sky (T/Lt. G.O.T.D. Henderson, RNVR) which had just joined.
HMS Vitality and HMS Milford continued on northwards. (72)

24 Aug 1944 Around 1200 OP/24, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN), HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and their escort, HMS Northern Sky (T/Lt. G.O.T.D. Henderson, RNVR), arrived at St.Johns from the U.K. (70)

Smyth, RCNR). It is possible the frigate HMCS Joliette (T/A/Lt.Cdr. G.N. Downey, RCNR) and / or the corvette HMCS Chambly (T/Lt. S.D. Taylor, RCNR) also participated. (72)
